docs.title: Home
docs.text: A secure, zero-CVE, and fully FIPS 140-3 compliant Java environment built for modern Kubernetes and Docker infrastructure.

docs.title: Wolfi Java FIPS
docs.text: High-Assurance Cryptographic Foundation for Cloud-Native Workloads

docs.title: Core Architecture
docs.text:
• Zero-CVE Base (Wolfi OS) --- Built on top of the Wolfi undistro. Designed for the container era to provide a minimalist attack surface with rolling updates, ensuring continuous Zero-CVE compliance.

  • Strict FIPS 140-3 Enforcement --- Integrated with Bouncy Castle FIPS (BC-FJA). Hardcoded at the JVM level (approved_only=true) to strictly reject any non-approved cryptographic algorithms (e.g., MD5).

  • Enterprise Java (Adoptium) --- Powered by Eclipse Temurin upstream binaries. Delivering rock-solid performance, stability, and seamless compatibility for Java 8, 11, 17, 21, and 25 LTS.

docs.title: Artifact Tiers
docs.text:
We provide three distinct image flavors for every supported Java version, ensuring you have the exact toolset required for your stage in the SDLC:

  • Production Distroless --- jre_distroless The ultimate production target. Contains only the JRE and strictly required libraries. Zero shell, zero package manager, zero system utilities.

  • Standard Image --- jre_standard The operational production target. Contains the JRE along with a basic shell (ash) and minimal utilities required for debugging and orchestration hooks.

  • Development SDK --- jdk_standard The builder environment. Contains the full JDK (compilers, tools), shell, and package manager (apk). Designed strictly for CI/CD pipelines.

docs.title: Continuous Validation
docs.text:
Every image in this repository undergoes rigorous static analysis and compliance validation before publishing.

  • Vulnerability Scanning: Deep inspection of all layers using Trivy.
  • Docker CIS: Automated validation against CIS Docker Benchmarks.
  • NSA K8s Hardening: Verified against NSA/CISA Container build guidelines.
  • K8s PSS Restricted: Architected to pass Kubernetes Pod Security Standards.

docs.title: Usage & Execution
docs.text:
All images in this repository share a unified and streamlined execution model. The ENTRYPOINT is strictly set to ["/opt/java/bin/java"]. This means the container behaves exactly like the native java executable—you simply pass your JVM arguments or .jar files directly.

Zero-Config Strict FIPS 140-3 Enforcement

You do not need to manually configure FIPS properties. We have injected the stringent security requirements directly into the image's environment via JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS.

By default, the JVM runs with -Dorg.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only=true. This guarantees that if your application attempts to invoke a non-compliant cryptographic algorithm (e.g., MD5 or DES), the Bouncy Castle provider will explicitly block it and throw a runtime exception, ensuring 100% cryptographic compliance without altering your application code.

docs.title: Implementation Examples
Cryptographic Guardrails & FIPS Enforcement
docs.text:
To achieve FIPS 140-3 compliance and Zero-Trust networking, this image enforces a "Hardened-by-Default" policy. Below is a breakdown of the security guardrails verified during the runtime trace:

Security Policy Breakdown

  • Strict EKU Validation (checkEKU: true): We explicitly verify that the server's certificate is intended for Server Authentication. Any certificate lacking this metadata or used for the wrong purpose (e.g., a code-signing cert used for HTTPS) is rejected immediately to prevent impersonation attacks.
  • No Assumptions Policy (assumeEKU: false): Unlike standard Java distributions, we do not "assume" a certificate is valid if the Extended Key Usage (EKU) field is missing. This forces all internal and external services to use professionally issued, well-defined certificates.
  • FIPS-Approved KeyStore (BCFKS): The traditional JKS and PKCS12 formats are bypassed for certificate management. We use the BCFKS (Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore) format, which uses FIPS-approved algorithms (like AES-CCM and SHA-512) to protect your root certificates (cacerts).
  • Algorithm Blacklisting: Weak protocols and ciphers are strictly disabled at the JVM level.
    • Blocked Protocols: SSLv3, TLSv1.0, TLSv1.1.
    • Blocked Ciphers: RC4, DES, 3DES, MD5, and DH/RSA keys smaller than 2048-bit.

docs.text: The runtime successfully established a TLS 1.3 session with adoptium.net using only FIPS-approved primitives, confirming that your application can safely communicate with modern APIs without compromising its cryptographic boundary.

docs.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation Tests
docs.text:
FIPS COMPLIANT Module BC-FJA is active and enforcing Approved Mode.

  • Total Tests --- 32
  • Passed --- 32
  • Failed --- 0
  • Time --- 75.61s

Our high-assurance validation lifecycle ensures every artifact meets uncompromising FIPS 140-3 standards. We compile our security suite using a trusted JDK before mounting it into a hardened, isolated JRE runtime. Within this boundary, Bouncy Castle FIPS is strictly injected and set to "Approved Only" mode to block legacy primitives. The engine then executes rigorous positive and negative assertions to verify cryptographic enforcement in real-time. This continuous auditing provides a zero-trust foundation for your mission-critical Java workloads.

docs.title: Cryptographic Testing Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Compilation Stage"
    A[Java Test Suite] -->|JDK javac| B(Validated Bytecode)
    end

    subgraph "Execution Boundary"
    B -->|Mount| C[Target JRE Image]
    D[BCFIPS Provider] -->|Inject| C
    E[Strict Policy] -->|approved_only=true| C
    end

    subgraph "Analysis"
    C -->|Run| F{Assert Security}
    F -->|Success| G[FIPS Verified]
    F -->|Violation| H[Security Breach]
    end

    style G fill:#00c853,color:#fff
    style H fill:#d50000,color:#fff
    style C stroke-width:4px

docs.title: Diagnostics Log
docs.text:
Verify NULL Encryption Cipher Suites are rejected 2.473s

Confirms the total rejection of NULL cipher suites. Any attempt to establish a network connection without encryption is a severe security violation and is blocked by the FIPS boundary.

Verify RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 Encryption is rejected 2.225s

Verifies rejection of RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding for encryption. Under strict FIPS enforcement, modern and secure padding schemes like OAEP are required, and legacy schemes are disabled.

Verify SHA-256 is allowed by BCFIPS 1.898s

Ensures the SHA-256 hash algorithm is functioning correctly. SHA-256 is a core FIPS-approved primitive used for secure message digesting and integrity verification.

Verify SHA1PRNG is rejected by BCFIPS 1.978s

Ensures that the legacy SHA1PRNG algorithm is strictly prohibited and inaccessible through the BCFIPS provider. FIPS 140-3 standards mandate the use of stronger, approved DRBG mechanisms and forbid the use of non-compliant RNG algorithms.

Verify BCJSSE is the mandated SSLContext provider 2.627s

Validates that the default SSLContext is using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This configuration ensures that all JVM-wide network operations utilize the FIPS-validated cryptographic module.

Verify RSA 2048-bit Key Generation is allowed 2.369s

Validates the generation of 2048-bit RSA key pairs. This confirms the provider can create asymmetric keys that meet the minimum security strength requirements defined by FIPS 140-3.

Verify ECDH Key Agreement using P-256 2.16s

Validates the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key agreement protocol. This confirms that the environment can securely establish shared secrets using approved elliptic curve primitives.

Verify Short HMAC Key is rejected 1.918s

Checks that HMAC operations reject keys that are shorter than the minimum required length. This ensures the integrity of the message authentication code meets FIPS security strength requirements.

Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 are strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 2.531s

Ensures that legacy protocols such as TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are strictly prohibited. These versions are no longer compliant with FIPS 140-3 standards due to known cryptographic weaknesses and vulnerabilities.

Verify 1024-bit RSA is rejected 1.865s

Validates the enforcement of minimum key lengths for RSA. FIPS 140-3 requires a minimum of 2048 bits; attempts to use 1024-bit or smaller keys must be blocked.

Verify SecureRandom uses FIPS-Approved DRBG 2.031s

Confirms that the default SecureRandom implementation utilizes the Bouncy Castle FIPS-approved DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator). This ensures all entropy and random value generation within the JVM meets the strict NIST SP 800-90A security requirements.

Verify AES-GCM is allowed by BCFIPS 1.906s

Verifies that AES in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) is available and operational. AES-GCM is a FIPS-approved authenticated encryption algorithm that provides both confidentiality and data integrity.

Verify MD5 is strictly rejected 1.953s

Verifies that the MD5 message digest algorithm is strictly prohibited. As a non-approved hash function in FIPS 140-3, any attempt to instantiate MD5 must result in a security exception.

Verify MD4 is strictly rejected 1.948s

Confirms that the MD4 hash algorithm is completely disabled. MD4 is cryptographically broken and strictly forbidden in any FIPS-validated environment.

Verify Non-NIST Curve (secp160r1) is rejected 2.027s

Ensures that only NIST-approved Elliptic Curves (e.g., P-256, P-384) are allowed. Attempts to use non-standard or custom curves must be rejected by the provider.

Verify AES-CBC with PKCS7 Padding 1.954s

Verifies that AES in Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode is available. CBC remains a FIPS-approved encryption mode for various legacy and standard interoperability requirements.

Verify Default KeyStore type is BCFKS for TLS 1.024s

Ensures that BCFKS is mandated as the default KeyStore type for JSSE operations. This prevents the accidental use of non-compliant storage formats like JKS or PKCS12 for managing trusted certificates.

Verify BouncyCastle FIPS is the primary security provider 1.964s

Checks the security provider chain to confirm that 'BCFIPS' is positioned at the highest priority. This configuration is critical to ensure the JVM uses the FIPS-validated cryptographic module for all operations and prevents accidental fallback to standard, non-certified providers.

Verify EC P-256 Key Generation is allowed 1.991s

Confirms successful generation of Elliptic Curve keys using the NIST P-256 curve. This curve is an approved standard for secure and efficient asymmetric cryptography in FIPS environments.

Verify Triple-DES Encryption is rejected 2.018s

Verifies that Triple-DES (TDEA) encryption is prohibited. Following recent NIST guidance, 3DES is no longer an approved encryption algorithm due to its vulnerability to Sweet32 attacks.

Verify JVM starts strictly in FIPS Approved Mode 10.93s

Ensures the JVM is operating in a strict FIPS-approved state by verifying the 'org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only' system property. This enforcement guarantees that any attempt to use non-FIPS compliant algorithms will be rejected at runtime.

Verify BCFKS Keystore is allowed and functional 1.946s

Verifies that the Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore (BCFKS) is fully supported and operational. BCFKS is the mandated storage format for keys and certificates within a FIPS 140-3 environment to ensure the protection of sensitive security parameters using approved algorithms.

Verify TLS 1.3 Handshake using BCFIPS Provider 2.764s

Validates successful TLS 1.3 handshake using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This confirms that modern, secure protocol standards are operational within the FIPS cryptographic boundary and utilize approved cipher suites.

Verify PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 is allowed 1.961s

Ensures that PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) is available. It verifies that secure cryptographic keys can be derived from passwords using FIPS-approved iteration and hashing methods.

Verify DES is strictly rejected 1.901s

Ensures that the legacy DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm is rejected. FIPS mode prohibits weak block ciphers with 56-bit keys to prevent brute-force vulnerabilities.

Verify 1024-bit DSA Key Generation is rejected 1.888s

Ensures that 1024-bit DSA keys are rejected. FIPS compliance mandates higher security strengths, effectively blocking legacy DSA parameters that do not meet the 112-bit security threshold.

Verify PBKDF2 with short salt (<128 bits) is rejected 1.999s

Validates that PBKDF2 operations require a minimum salt length. FIPS standards enforce sufficient entropy in key derivation to protect against pre-computed dictionary attacks.

Verify RC4 Cipher Suites are strictly rejected 2.582s

Verifies that RC4-based cipher suites are rejected at the JSSE level. RC4 is a broken stream cipher and is strictly prohibited in FIPS environments to maintain data confidentiality.

Verify JKS Keystore is strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 1.979s

Ensures that legacy Java KeyStore (JKS) files are strictly rejected at runtime. Blocking non-compliant keystore formats is a mandatory security control to prevent the accidental use of weak integrity checks and non-approved cryptographic primitives.

Verify HMAC-SHA256 is allowed 1.97s

No description provided

Verify SHA-1 Signature Generation is rejected 2.392s

Confirms that SHA-1 based digital signatures are rejected for creation. FIPS policy prohibits SHA-1 for digital signature generation due to collision risks.

Verify Anonymous Cipher Suites (DH_anon) are rejected 2.437s

Ensures that anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites are disabled. These suites fail to provide server authentication and are explicitly forbidden in FIPS mode to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

docs.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation Tests
docs.text:
FIPS COMPLIANT Module BC-FJA is active and enforcing Approved Mode.

  • Total Tests --- 32
  • Passed --- 32
  • Failed --- 0
  • Time --- 72.08s

Our high-assurance validation lifecycle ensures every artifact meets uncompromising FIPS 140-3 standards. We compile our security suite using a trusted JDK before mounting it into a hardened, isolated JRE runtime. Within this boundary, Bouncy Castle FIPS is strictly injected and set to "Approved Only" mode to block legacy primitives. The engine then executes rigorous positive and negative assertions to verify cryptographic enforcement in real-time. This continuous auditing provides a zero-trust foundation for your mission-critical Java workloads.

docs.title: Cryptographic Testing Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Compilation Stage"
    A[Java Test Suite] -->|JDK javac| B(Validated Bytecode)
    end

    subgraph "Execution Boundary"
    B -->|Mount| C[Target JRE Image]
    D[BCFIPS Provider] -->|Inject| C
    E[Strict Policy] -->|approved_only=true| C
    end

    subgraph "Analysis"
    C -->|Run| F{Assert Security}
    F -->|Success| G[FIPS Verified]
    F -->|Violation| H[Security Breach]
    end

    style G fill:#00c853,color:#fff
    style H fill:#d50000,color:#fff
    style C stroke-width:4px

docs.title: Diagnostics Log
docs.text:
Verify RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 Encryption is rejected 2.309s

Verifies rejection of RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding for encryption. Under strict FIPS enforcement, modern and secure padding schemes like OAEP are required, and legacy schemes are disabled.

Verify MD5 is strictly rejected 1.86s

Verifies that the MD5 message digest algorithm is strictly prohibited. As a non-approved hash function in FIPS 140-3, any attempt to instantiate MD5 must result in a security exception.

Verify BouncyCastle FIPS is the primary security provider 1.953s

Checks the security provider chain to confirm that 'BCFIPS' is positioned at the highest priority. This configuration is critical to ensure the JVM uses the FIPS-validated cryptographic module for all operations and prevents accidental fallback to standard, non-certified providers.

Verify DES is strictly rejected 1.912s

Ensures that the legacy DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm is rejected. FIPS mode prohibits weak block ciphers with 56-bit keys to prevent brute-force vulnerabilities.

Verify Anonymous Cipher Suites (DH_anon) are rejected 2.51s

Ensures that anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites are disabled. These suites fail to provide server authentication and are explicitly forbidden in FIPS mode to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

Verify RSA 2048-bit Key Generation is allowed 2.11s

Validates the generation of 2048-bit RSA key pairs. This confirms the provider can create asymmetric keys that meet the minimum security strength requirements defined by FIPS 140-3.

Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 are strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 2.394s

Ensures that legacy protocols such as TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are strictly prohibited. These versions are no longer compliant with FIPS 140-3 standards due to known cryptographic weaknesses and vulnerabilities.

Verify EC P-256 Key Generation is allowed 1.912s

Confirms successful generation of Elliptic Curve keys using the NIST P-256 curve. This curve is an approved standard for secure and efficient asymmetric cryptography in FIPS environments.

Verify ECDH Key Agreement using P-256 2.132s

Validates the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key agreement protocol. This confirms that the environment can securely establish shared secrets using approved elliptic curve primitives.

Verify AES-CBC with PKCS7 Padding 2.007s

Verifies that AES in Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode is available. CBC remains a FIPS-approved encryption mode for various legacy and standard interoperability requirements.

Verify RC4 Cipher Suites are strictly rejected 2.583s

Verifies that RC4-based cipher suites are rejected at the JSSE level. RC4 is a broken stream cipher and is strictly prohibited in FIPS environments to maintain data confidentiality.

Verify HMAC-SHA256 is allowed 1.928s

No description provided

Verify SHA-1 Signature Generation is rejected 2.294s

Confirms that SHA-1 based digital signatures are rejected for creation. FIPS policy prohibits SHA-1 for digital signature generation due to collision risks.

Verify PBKDF2 with short salt (<128 bits) is rejected 1.906s

Validates that PBKDF2 operations require a minimum salt length. FIPS standards enforce sufficient entropy in key derivation to protect against pre-computed dictionary attacks.

Verify TLS 1.3 Handshake using BCFIPS Provider 2.718s

Validates successful TLS 1.3 handshake using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This confirms that modern, secure protocol standards are operational within the FIPS cryptographic boundary and utilize approved cipher suites.

Verify BCFKS Keystore is allowed and functional 1.924s

Verifies that the Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore (BCFKS) is fully supported and operational. BCFKS is the mandated storage format for keys and certificates within a FIPS 140-3 environment to ensure the protection of sensitive security parameters using approved algorithms.

Verify Short HMAC Key is rejected 1.932s

Checks that HMAC operations reject keys that are shorter than the minimum required length. This ensures the integrity of the message authentication code meets FIPS security strength requirements.

Verify Triple-DES Encryption is rejected 1.981s

Verifies that Triple-DES (TDEA) encryption is prohibited. Following recent NIST guidance, 3DES is no longer an approved encryption algorithm due to its vulnerability to Sweet32 attacks.

Verify 1024-bit DSA Key Generation is rejected 1.999s

Ensures that 1024-bit DSA keys are rejected. FIPS compliance mandates higher security strengths, effectively blocking legacy DSA parameters that do not meet the 112-bit security threshold.

Verify 1024-bit RSA is rejected 1.853s

Validates the enforcement of minimum key lengths for RSA. FIPS 140-3 requires a minimum of 2048 bits; attempts to use 1024-bit or smaller keys must be blocked.

Verify SecureRandom uses FIPS-Approved DRBG 1.923s

Confirms that the default SecureRandom implementation utilizes the Bouncy Castle FIPS-approved DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator). This ensures all entropy and random value generation within the JVM meets the strict NIST SP 800-90A security requirements.

Verify BCJSSE is the mandated SSLContext provider 2.561s

Validates that the default SSLContext is using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This configuration ensures that all JVM-wide network operations utilize the FIPS-validated cryptographic module.

Verify JVM starts strictly in FIPS Approved Mode 8.481s

Ensures the JVM is operating in a strict FIPS-approved state by verifying the 'org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only' system property. This enforcement guarantees that any attempt to use non-FIPS compliant algorithms will be rejected at runtime.

Verify AES-GCM is allowed by BCFIPS 1.888s

Verifies that AES in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) is available and operational. AES-GCM is a FIPS-approved authenticated encryption algorithm that provides both confidentiality and data integrity.

Verify NULL Encryption Cipher Suites are rejected 2.575s

Confirms the total rejection of NULL cipher suites. Any attempt to establish a network connection without encryption is a severe security violation and is blocked by the FIPS boundary.

Verify SHA1PRNG is rejected by BCFIPS 1.878s

Ensures that the legacy SHA1PRNG algorithm is strictly prohibited and inaccessible through the BCFIPS provider. FIPS 140-3 standards mandate the use of stronger, approved DRBG mechanisms and forbid the use of non-compliant RNG algorithms.

Verify Non-NIST Curve (secp160r1) is rejected 1.986s

Ensures that only NIST-approved Elliptic Curves (e.g., P-256, P-384) are allowed. Attempts to use non-standard or custom curves must be rejected by the provider.

Verify Default KeyStore type is BCFKS for TLS 0.989s

Ensures that BCFKS is mandated as the default KeyStore type for JSSE operations. This prevents the accidental use of non-compliant storage formats like JKS or PKCS12 for managing trusted certificates.

Verify JKS Keystore is strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 1.944s

Ensures that legacy Java KeyStore (JKS) files are strictly rejected at runtime. Blocking non-compliant keystore formats is a mandatory security control to prevent the accidental use of weak integrity checks and non-approved cryptographic primitives.

Verify MD4 is strictly rejected 1.901s

Confirms that the MD4 hash algorithm is completely disabled. MD4 is cryptographically broken and strictly forbidden in any FIPS-validated environment.

Verify PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 is allowed 1.867s

Ensures that PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) is available. It verifies that secure cryptographic keys can be derived from passwords using FIPS-approved iteration and hashing methods.

Verify SHA-256 is allowed by BCFIPS 1.87s

Ensures the SHA-256 hash algorithm is functioning correctly. SHA-256 is a core FIPS-approved primitive used for secure message digesting and integrity verification.

docs.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation Tests
docs.text:
FIPS COMPLIANT Module BC-FJA is active and enforcing Approved Mode.

  • Total Tests --- 32
  • Passed --- 32
  • Failed --- 0
  • Time --- 68.78s

Our high-assurance validation lifecycle ensures every artifact meets uncompromising FIPS 140-3 standards. We compile our security suite using a trusted JDK before mounting it into a hardened, isolated JRE runtime. Within this boundary, Bouncy Castle FIPS is strictly injected and set to "Approved Only" mode to block legacy primitives. The engine then executes rigorous positive and negative assertions to verify cryptographic enforcement in real-time. This continuous auditing provides a zero-trust foundation for your mission-critical Java workloads.

docs.title: Cryptographic Testing Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Compilation Stage"
    A[Java Test Suite] -->|JDK javac| B(Validated Bytecode)
    end

    subgraph "Execution Boundary"
    B -->|Mount| C[Target JRE Image]
    D[BCFIPS Provider] -->|Inject| C
    E[Strict Policy] -->|approved_only=true| C
    end

    subgraph "Analysis"
    C -->|Run| F{Assert Security}
    F -->|Success| G[FIPS Verified]
    F -->|Violation| H[Security Breach]
    end

    style G fill:#00c853,color:#fff
    style H fill:#d50000,color:#fff
    style C stroke-width:4px

docs.title: Diagnostics Log
docs.text:
Verify RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 Encryption is rejected 2.282s

Verifies rejection of RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding for encryption. Under strict FIPS enforcement, modern and secure padding schemes like OAEP are required, and legacy schemes are disabled.

Verify NULL Encryption Cipher Suites are rejected 2.505s

Confirms the total rejection of NULL cipher suites. Any attempt to establish a network connection without encryption is a severe security violation and is blocked by the FIPS boundary.

Verify Short HMAC Key is rejected 1.794s

Checks that HMAC operations reject keys that are shorter than the minimum required length. This ensures the integrity of the message authentication code meets FIPS security strength requirements.

Verify MD4 is strictly rejected 1.893s

Confirms that the MD4 hash algorithm is completely disabled. MD4 is cryptographically broken and strictly forbidden in any FIPS-validated environment.

Verify 1024-bit DSA Key Generation is rejected 1.818s

Ensures that 1024-bit DSA keys are rejected. FIPS compliance mandates higher security strengths, effectively blocking legacy DSA parameters that do not meet the 112-bit security threshold.

Verify BouncyCastle FIPS is the primary security provider 1.86s

Checks the security provider chain to confirm that 'BCFIPS' is positioned at the highest priority. This configuration is critical to ensure the JVM uses the FIPS-validated cryptographic module for all operations and prevents accidental fallback to standard, non-certified providers.

Verify TLS 1.3 Handshake using BCFIPS Provider 2.531s

Validates successful TLS 1.3 handshake using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This confirms that modern, secure protocol standards are operational within the FIPS cryptographic boundary and utilize approved cipher suites.

Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 are strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 2.465s

Ensures that legacy protocols such as TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are strictly prohibited. These versions are no longer compliant with FIPS 140-3 standards due to known cryptographic weaknesses and vulnerabilities.

Verify BCJSSE is the mandated SSLContext provider 2.607s

Validates that the default SSLContext is using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This configuration ensures that all JVM-wide network operations utilize the FIPS-validated cryptographic module.

Verify BCFKS Keystore is allowed and functional 1.75s

Verifies that the Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore (BCFKS) is fully supported and operational. BCFKS is the mandated storage format for keys and certificates within a FIPS 140-3 environment to ensure the protection of sensitive security parameters using approved algorithms.

Verify Default KeyStore type is BCFKS for TLS 0.962s

Ensures that BCFKS is mandated as the default KeyStore type for JSSE operations. This prevents the accidental use of non-compliant storage formats like JKS or PKCS12 for managing trusted certificates.

Verify RC4 Cipher Suites are strictly rejected 2.541s

Verifies that RC4-based cipher suites are rejected at the JSSE level. RC4 is a broken stream cipher and is strictly prohibited in FIPS environments to maintain data confidentiality.

Verify SecureRandom uses FIPS-Approved DRBG 1.838s

Confirms that the default SecureRandom implementation utilizes the Bouncy Castle FIPS-approved DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator). This ensures all entropy and random value generation within the JVM meets the strict NIST SP 800-90A security requirements.

Verify AES-CBC with PKCS7 Padding 1.874s

Verifies that AES in Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode is available. CBC remains a FIPS-approved encryption mode for various legacy and standard interoperability requirements.

Verify 1024-bit RSA is rejected 1.861s

Validates the enforcement of minimum key lengths for RSA. FIPS 140-3 requires a minimum of 2048 bits; attempts to use 1024-bit or smaller keys must be blocked.

Verify MD5 is strictly rejected 1.871s

Verifies that the MD5 message digest algorithm is strictly prohibited. As a non-approved hash function in FIPS 140-3, any attempt to instantiate MD5 must result in a security exception.

Verify HMAC-SHA256 is allowed 1.77s

No description provided

Verify Non-NIST Curve (secp160r1) is rejected 1.943s

Ensures that only NIST-approved Elliptic Curves (e.g., P-256, P-384) are allowed. Attempts to use non-standard or custom curves must be rejected by the provider.

Verify JVM starts strictly in FIPS Approved Mode 7.531s

Ensures the JVM is operating in a strict FIPS-approved state by verifying the 'org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only' system property. This enforcement guarantees that any attempt to use non-FIPS compliant algorithms will be rejected at runtime.

Verify DES is strictly rejected 1.818s

Ensures that the legacy DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm is rejected. FIPS mode prohibits weak block ciphers with 56-bit keys to prevent brute-force vulnerabilities.

Verify SHA1PRNG is rejected by BCFIPS 1.888s

Ensures that the legacy SHA1PRNG algorithm is strictly prohibited and inaccessible through the BCFIPS provider. FIPS 140-3 standards mandate the use of stronger, approved DRBG mechanisms and forbid the use of non-compliant RNG algorithms.

Verify SHA-1 Signature Generation is rejected 1.959s

Confirms that SHA-1 based digital signatures are rejected for creation. FIPS policy prohibits SHA-1 for digital signature generation due to collision risks.

Verify EC P-256 Key Generation is allowed 1.786s

Confirms successful generation of Elliptic Curve keys using the NIST P-256 curve. This curve is an approved standard for secure and efficient asymmetric cryptography in FIPS environments.

Verify RSA 2048-bit Key Generation is allowed 2.22s

Validates the generation of 2048-bit RSA key pairs. This confirms the provider can create asymmetric keys that meet the minimum security strength requirements defined by FIPS 140-3.

Verify PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 is allowed 1.91s

Ensures that PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) is available. It verifies that secure cryptographic keys can be derived from passwords using FIPS-approved iteration and hashing methods.

Verify JKS Keystore is strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 1.823s

Ensures that legacy Java KeyStore (JKS) files are strictly rejected at runtime. Blocking non-compliant keystore formats is a mandatory security control to prevent the accidental use of weak integrity checks and non-approved cryptographic primitives.

Verify SHA-256 is allowed by BCFIPS 1.698s

Ensures the SHA-256 hash algorithm is functioning correctly. SHA-256 is a core FIPS-approved primitive used for secure message digesting and integrity verification.

Verify Anonymous Cipher Suites (DH_anon) are rejected 2.584s

Ensures that anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites are disabled. These suites fail to provide server authentication and are explicitly forbidden in FIPS mode to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

Verify Triple-DES Encryption is rejected 1.814s

Verifies that Triple-DES (TDEA) encryption is prohibited. Following recent NIST guidance, 3DES is no longer an approved encryption algorithm due to its vulnerability to Sweet32 attacks.

Verify ECDH Key Agreement using P-256 1.992s

Validates the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key agreement protocol. This confirms that the environment can securely establish shared secrets using approved elliptic curve primitives.

Verify AES-GCM is allowed by BCFIPS 1.81s

Verifies that AES in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) is available and operational. AES-GCM is a FIPS-approved authenticated encryption algorithm that provides both confidentiality and data integrity.

Verify PBKDF2 with short salt (<128 bits) is rejected 1.777s

Validates that PBKDF2 operations require a minimum salt length. FIPS standards enforce sufficient entropy in key derivation to protect against pre-computed dictionary attacks.

docs.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation Tests
docs.text:
FIPS COMPLIANT Module BC-FJA is active and enforcing Approved Mode.

  • Total Tests --- 32
  • Passed --- 32
  • Failed --- 0
  • Time --- 71.43s

Our high-assurance validation lifecycle ensures every artifact meets uncompromising FIPS 140-3 standards. We compile our security suite using a trusted JDK before mounting it into a hardened, isolated JRE runtime. Within this boundary, Bouncy Castle FIPS is strictly injected and set to "Approved Only" mode to block legacy primitives. The engine then executes rigorous positive and negative assertions to verify cryptographic enforcement in real-time. This continuous auditing provides a zero-trust foundation for your mission-critical Java workloads.

docs.title: Cryptographic Testing Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Compilation Stage"
    A[Java Test Suite] -->|JDK javac| B(Validated Bytecode)
    end

    subgraph "Execution Boundary"
    B -->|Mount| C[Target JRE Image]
    D[BCFIPS Provider] -->|Inject| C
    E[Strict Policy] -->|approved_only=true| C
    end

    subgraph "Analysis"
    C -->|Run| F{Assert Security}
    F -->|Success| G[FIPS Verified]
    F -->|Violation| H[Security Breach]
    end

    style G fill:#00c853,color:#fff
    style H fill:#d50000,color:#fff
    style C stroke-width:4px

docs.title: Diagnostics Log
docs.text:
Verify SHA1PRNG is rejected by BCFIPS 1.924s

Ensures that the legacy SHA1PRNG algorithm is strictly prohibited and inaccessible through the BCFIPS provider. FIPS 140-3 standards mandate the use of stronger, approved DRBG mechanisms and forbid the use of non-compliant RNG algorithms.

Verify AES-GCM is allowed by BCFIPS 1.938s

Verifies that AES in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) is available and operational. AES-GCM is a FIPS-approved authenticated encryption algorithm that provides both confidentiality and data integrity.

Verify 1024-bit RSA is rejected 1.885s

Validates the enforcement of minimum key lengths for RSA. FIPS 140-3 requires a minimum of 2048 bits; attempts to use 1024-bit or smaller keys must be blocked.

Verify PBKDF2 with short salt (<128 bits) is rejected 1.901s

Validates that PBKDF2 operations require a minimum salt length. FIPS standards enforce sufficient entropy in key derivation to protect against pre-computed dictionary attacks.

Verify AES-CBC with PKCS7 Padding 1.966s

Verifies that AES in Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode is available. CBC remains a FIPS-approved encryption mode for various legacy and standard interoperability requirements.

Verify ECDH Key Agreement using P-256 2.056s

Validates the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key agreement protocol. This confirms that the environment can securely establish shared secrets using approved elliptic curve primitives.

Verify Short HMAC Key is rejected 1.851s

Checks that HMAC operations reject keys that are shorter than the minimum required length. This ensures the integrity of the message authentication code meets FIPS security strength requirements.

Verify PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 is allowed 1.833s

Ensures that PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) is available. It verifies that secure cryptographic keys can be derived from passwords using FIPS-approved iteration and hashing methods.

Verify BCJSSE is the mandated SSLContext provider 2.497s

Validates that the default SSLContext is using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This configuration ensures that all JVM-wide network operations utilize the FIPS-validated cryptographic module.

Verify RC4 Cipher Suites are strictly rejected 2.338s

Verifies that RC4-based cipher suites are rejected at the JSSE level. RC4 is a broken stream cipher and is strictly prohibited in FIPS environments to maintain data confidentiality.

Verify JKS Keystore is strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 1.837s

Ensures that legacy Java KeyStore (JKS) files are strictly rejected at runtime. Blocking non-compliant keystore formats is a mandatory security control to prevent the accidental use of weak integrity checks and non-approved cryptographic primitives.

Verify JVM starts strictly in FIPS Approved Mode 9.39s

Ensures the JVM is operating in a strict FIPS-approved state by verifying the 'org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only' system property. This enforcement guarantees that any attempt to use non-FIPS compliant algorithms will be rejected at runtime.

Verify BouncyCastle FIPS is the primary security provider 2.017s

Checks the security provider chain to confirm that 'BCFIPS' is positioned at the highest priority. This configuration is critical to ensure the JVM uses the FIPS-validated cryptographic module for all operations and prevents accidental fallback to standard, non-certified providers.

Verify Default KeyStore type is BCFKS for TLS 1.02s

Ensures that BCFKS is mandated as the default KeyStore type for JSSE operations. This prevents the accidental use of non-compliant storage formats like JKS or PKCS12 for managing trusted certificates.

Verify 1024-bit DSA Key Generation is rejected 1.789s

Ensures that 1024-bit DSA keys are rejected. FIPS compliance mandates higher security strengths, effectively blocking legacy DSA parameters that do not meet the 112-bit security threshold.

Verify RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 Encryption is rejected 2.202s

Verifies rejection of RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding for encryption. Under strict FIPS enforcement, modern and secure padding schemes like OAEP are required, and legacy schemes are disabled.

Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 are strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 2.563s

Ensures that legacy protocols such as TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are strictly prohibited. These versions are no longer compliant with FIPS 140-3 standards due to known cryptographic weaknesses and vulnerabilities.

Verify HMAC-SHA256 is allowed 1.834s

No description provided

Verify MD4 is strictly rejected 1.861s

Confirms that the MD4 hash algorithm is completely disabled. MD4 is cryptographically broken and strictly forbidden in any FIPS-validated environment.

Verify TLS 1.3 Handshake using BCFIPS Provider 2.699s

Validates successful TLS 1.3 handshake using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This confirms that modern, secure protocol standards are operational within the FIPS cryptographic boundary and utilize approved cipher suites.

Verify Triple-DES Encryption is rejected 1.879s

Verifies that Triple-DES (TDEA) encryption is prohibited. Following recent NIST guidance, 3DES is no longer an approved encryption algorithm due to its vulnerability to Sweet32 attacks.

Verify Non-NIST Curve (secp160r1) is rejected 1.882s

Ensures that only NIST-approved Elliptic Curves (e.g., P-256, P-384) are allowed. Attempts to use non-standard or custom curves must be rejected by the provider.

Verify EC P-256 Key Generation is allowed 1.872s

Confirms successful generation of Elliptic Curve keys using the NIST P-256 curve. This curve is an approved standard for secure and efficient asymmetric cryptography in FIPS environments.

Verify NULL Encryption Cipher Suites are rejected 2.582s

Confirms the total rejection of NULL cipher suites. Any attempt to establish a network connection without encryption is a severe security violation and is blocked by the FIPS boundary.

Verify BCFKS Keystore is allowed and functional 1.873s

Verifies that the Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore (BCFKS) is fully supported and operational. BCFKS is the mandated storage format for keys and certificates within a FIPS 140-3 environment to ensure the protection of sensitive security parameters using approved algorithms.

Verify RSA 2048-bit Key Generation is allowed 2.026s

Validates the generation of 2048-bit RSA key pairs. This confirms the provider can create asymmetric keys that meet the minimum security strength requirements defined by FIPS 140-3.

Verify MD5 is strictly rejected 1.889s

Verifies that the MD5 message digest algorithm is strictly prohibited. As a non-approved hash function in FIPS 140-3, any attempt to instantiate MD5 must result in a security exception.

Verify DES is strictly rejected 1.802s

Ensures that the legacy DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm is rejected. FIPS mode prohibits weak block ciphers with 56-bit keys to prevent brute-force vulnerabilities.

Verify SecureRandom uses FIPS-Approved DRBG 1.843s

Confirms that the default SecureRandom implementation utilizes the Bouncy Castle FIPS-approved DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator). This ensures all entropy and random value generation within the JVM meets the strict NIST SP 800-90A security requirements.

Verify SHA-1 Signature Generation is rejected 2.048s

Confirms that SHA-1 based digital signatures are rejected for creation. FIPS policy prohibits SHA-1 for digital signature generation due to collision risks.

Verify Anonymous Cipher Suites (DH_anon) are rejected 2.518s

Ensures that anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites are disabled. These suites fail to provide server authentication and are explicitly forbidden in FIPS mode to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

Verify SHA-256 is allowed by BCFIPS 1.81s

Ensures the SHA-256 hash algorithm is functioning correctly. SHA-256 is a core FIPS-approved primitive used for secure message digesting and integrity verification.

ID Hardening Control Severity Responsibility 3.0 Use CNI plugin that supports NetworkPolicy API (Manual) CRITICAL 5.0 Control plan disable insecure port (Manual) CRITICAL 6.0 Ensure kube config file permission (Manual) CRITICAL 8.0 Audit policy is configure (Manual) HIGH

Hardening Principles Applied: 1. Non-Root Execution: Container runs as a non-privileged user to limit exploit impact. 2. Verified Toolchain: All system components are sourced from the hardened Wolfi ecosystem. 3. SBOM Transparency: Full CycloneDX SBOM is provided for all included dependencies.

docs.text:

Why This Matters: 1. Isolation: Prevents access to host network or sensitive kernel namespaces. 2. Least Privilege: Ensuring build agents run as non-root prevents "Escape-to-Host" attacks. 3. Consistency: Matches the security posture of the production Distroless image.

docs.title: FIPS 140-3 Validation Tests
docs.text:
FIPS COMPLIANT Module BC-FJA is active and enforcing Approved Mode.

  • Total Tests --- 32
  • Passed --- 32
  • Failed --- 0
  • Time --- 66.02s

Our high-assurance validation lifecycle ensures every artifact meets uncompromising FIPS 140-3 standards. We compile our security suite using a trusted JDK before mounting it into a hardened, isolated JRE runtime. Within this boundary, Bouncy Castle FIPS is strictly injected and set to "Approved Only" mode to block legacy primitives. The engine then executes rigorous positive and negative assertions to verify cryptographic enforcement in real-time. This continuous auditing provides a zero-trust foundation for your mission-critical Java workloads.

docs.title: Cryptographic Testing Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph "Compilation Stage"
    A[Java Test Suite] -->|JDK javac| B(Validated Bytecode)
    end

    subgraph "Execution Boundary"
    B -->|Mount| C[Target JRE Image]
    D[BCFIPS Provider] -->|Inject| C
    E[Strict Policy] -->|approved_only=true| C
    end

    subgraph "Analysis"
    C -->|Run| F{Assert Security}
    F -->|Success| G[FIPS Verified]
    F -->|Violation| H[Security Breach]
    end

    style G fill:#00c853,color:#fff
    style H fill:#d50000,color:#fff
    style C stroke-width:4px

docs.title: Diagnostics Log
docs.text:
Verify MD4 is strictly rejected 1.867s

Confirms that the MD4 hash algorithm is completely disabled. MD4 is cryptographically broken and strictly forbidden in any FIPS-validated environment.

Verify BCFKS Keystore is allowed and functional 1.693s

Verifies that the Bouncy Castle FIPS KeyStore (BCFKS) is fully supported and operational. BCFKS is the mandated storage format for keys and certificates within a FIPS 140-3 environment to ensure the protection of sensitive security parameters using approved algorithms.

Verify Anonymous Cipher Suites (DH_anon) are rejected 2.503s

Ensures that anonymous Diffie-Hellman cipher suites are disabled. These suites fail to provide server authentication and are explicitly forbidden in FIPS mode to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.

Verify 1024-bit RSA is rejected 1.67s

Validates the enforcement of minimum key lengths for RSA. FIPS 140-3 requires a minimum of 2048 bits; attempts to use 1024-bit or smaller keys must be blocked.

Verify SHA1PRNG is rejected by BCFIPS 1.757s

Ensures that the legacy SHA1PRNG algorithm is strictly prohibited and inaccessible through the BCFIPS provider. FIPS 140-3 standards mandate the use of stronger, approved DRBG mechanisms and forbid the use of non-compliant RNG algorithms.

Verify JVM starts strictly in FIPS Approved Mode 6.273s

Ensures the JVM is operating in a strict FIPS-approved state by verifying the 'org.bouncycastle.fips.approved_only' system property. This enforcement guarantees that any attempt to use non-FIPS compliant algorithms will be rejected at runtime.

Verify EC P-256 Key Generation is allowed 1.906s

Confirms successful generation of Elliptic Curve keys using the NIST P-256 curve. This curve is an approved standard for secure and efficient asymmetric cryptography in FIPS environments.

Verify ECDH Key Agreement using P-256 1.951s

Validates the Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) key agreement protocol. This confirms that the environment can securely establish shared secrets using approved elliptic curve primitives.

Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 are strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 2.441s

Ensures that legacy protocols such as TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are strictly prohibited. These versions are no longer compliant with FIPS 140-3 standards due to known cryptographic weaknesses and vulnerabilities.

Verify MD5 is strictly rejected 1.693s

Verifies that the MD5 message digest algorithm is strictly prohibited. As a non-approved hash function in FIPS 140-3, any attempt to instantiate MD5 must result in a security exception.

Verify Non-NIST Curve (secp160r1) is rejected 1.948s

Ensures that only NIST-approved Elliptic Curves (e.g., P-256, P-384) are allowed. Attempts to use non-standard or custom curves must be rejected by the provider.

Verify DES is strictly rejected 1.716s

Ensures that the legacy DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm is rejected. FIPS mode prohibits weak block ciphers with 56-bit keys to prevent brute-force vulnerabilities.

Verify BouncyCastle FIPS is the primary security provider 1.764s

Checks the security provider chain to confirm that 'BCFIPS' is positioned at the highest priority. This configuration is critical to ensure the JVM uses the FIPS-validated cryptographic module for all operations and prevents accidental fallback to standard, non-certified providers.

Verify RSA 2048-bit Key Generation is allowed 1.894s

Validates the generation of 2048-bit RSA key pairs. This confirms the provider can create asymmetric keys that meet the minimum security strength requirements defined by FIPS 140-3.

Verify RC4 Cipher Suites are strictly rejected 2.386s

Verifies that RC4-based cipher suites are rejected at the JSSE level. RC4 is a broken stream cipher and is strictly prohibited in FIPS environments to maintain data confidentiality.

Verify TLS 1.3 Handshake using BCFIPS Provider 2.498s

Validates successful TLS 1.3 handshake using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This confirms that modern, secure protocol standards are operational within the FIPS cryptographic boundary and utilize approved cipher suites.

Verify Triple-DES Encryption is rejected 1.872s

Verifies that Triple-DES (TDEA) encryption is prohibited. Following recent NIST guidance, 3DES is no longer an approved encryption algorithm due to its vulnerability to Sweet32 attacks.

Verify SecureRandom uses FIPS-Approved DRBG 1.907s

Confirms that the default SecureRandom implementation utilizes the Bouncy Castle FIPS-approved DRBG (Deterministic Random Bit Generator). This ensures all entropy and random value generation within the JVM meets the strict NIST SP 800-90A security requirements.

Verify RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 Encryption is rejected 2.148s

Verifies rejection of RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 padding for encryption. Under strict FIPS enforcement, modern and secure padding schemes like OAEP are required, and legacy schemes are disabled.

Verify SHA-1 Signature Generation is rejected 1.847s

Confirms that SHA-1 based digital signatures are rejected for creation. FIPS policy prohibits SHA-1 for digital signature generation due to collision risks.

Verify PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256 is allowed 1.967s

Ensures that PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) is available. It verifies that secure cryptographic keys can be derived from passwords using FIPS-approved iteration and hashing methods.

Verify BCJSSE is the mandated SSLContext provider 2.491s

Validates that the default SSLContext is using the Bouncy Castle JSSE provider. This configuration ensures that all JVM-wide network operations utilize the FIPS-validated cryptographic module.

Verify SHA-256 is allowed by BCFIPS 1.685s

Ensures the SHA-256 hash algorithm is functioning correctly. SHA-256 is a core FIPS-approved primitive used for secure message digesting and integrity verification.

Verify AES-CBC with PKCS7 Padding 1.998s

Verifies that AES in Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) mode is available. CBC remains a FIPS-approved encryption mode for various legacy and standard interoperability requirements.

Verify Default KeyStore type is BCFKS for TLS 0.826s

Ensures that BCFKS is mandated as the default KeyStore type for JSSE operations. This prevents the accidental use of non-compliant storage formats like JKS or PKCS12 for managing trusted certificates.

Verify 1024-bit DSA Key Generation is rejected 1.685s

Ensures that 1024-bit DSA keys are rejected. FIPS compliance mandates higher security strengths, effectively blocking legacy DSA parameters that do not meet the 112-bit security threshold.

Verify NULL Encryption Cipher Suites are rejected 2.393s

Confirms the total rejection of NULL cipher suites. Any attempt to establish a network connection without encryption is a severe security violation and is blocked by the FIPS boundary.

Verify HMAC-SHA256 is allowed 1.876s

No description provided

Verify AES-GCM is allowed by BCFIPS 1.912s

Verifies that AES in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) is available and operational. AES-GCM is a FIPS-approved authenticated encryption algorithm that provides both confidentiality and data integrity.

Verify Short HMAC Key is rejected 1.831s

Checks that HMAC operations reject keys that are shorter than the minimum required length. This ensures the integrity of the message authentication code meets FIPS security strength requirements.

Verify PBKDF2 with short salt (<128 bits) is rejected 1.928s

Validates that PBKDF2 operations require a minimum salt length. FIPS standards enforce sufficient entropy in key derivation to protect against pre-computed dictionary attacks.

Verify JKS Keystore is strictly rejected in FIPS Mode 1.695s

Ensures that legacy Java KeyStore (JKS) files are strictly rejected at runtime. Blocking non-compliant keystore formats is a mandatory security control to prevent the accidental use of weak integrity checks and non-approved cryptographic primitives.
